step_by_step_reasoning: The transcript describes a Magento-based e-commerce platform run by Triboo. Users query Route 53 for DNS, and requests go through WAF to CloudFront. CloudFront routes static asset requests to S3 and dynamic requests to a custom load balancer (running on EC2). The custom load balancer forwards requests to an EKS cluster running Magento. EKS interacts with Aurora (MySQL backend), ElastiCache (sessions/caching), Elasticsearch (indexing), and EFS for shared file storage, while logs are saved to S3. For administrative access, developers connect via AWS VPN. A custom Mail Server runs on EC2, Redshift is used for BI, and Lambda handles monitoring, alerting, and configuration.

graph.name: Triboo Magento E-Commerce Platform Architecture
graph.notes: E-commerce platform architecture handling over 100 stores with containerized environments using AWS EKS and custom EC2 load balancing.

nodes.name: Customer
nodes.notes: Shoppers visiting the online stores managed by Triboo.

nodes.name: Route 53
nodes.notes: Handles external domains and internal FQDNs for service-to-service calls.

nodes.name: AWS WAF
nodes.notes: Protects against DDoS and bot attacks.

nodes.name: CloudFront CDN
nodes.notes: Caches static assets at the edge and handles request routing behaviors.

nodes.name: Static S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores images and other static assets.

nodes.name: Custom Load Balancer
nodes.notes: Runs 'pass secure' proprietary load balancing software on EC2 instances.

nodes.name: Magento EKS Cluster
nodes.notes: Runs containerized Magento storefronts, segregating search traffic and purchase traffic.

nodes.name: Amazon Aurora
nodes.notes: MySQL-compatible database for Magento relational data.

nodes.name: ElastiCache Redis
nodes.notes: Used for session storage and application caching.

nodes.name: Elasticsearch
nodes.notes: Used for product catalog indexing and search features.

nodes.name: EFS / Custom NFS
nodes.notes: Shared file system for media and assets (combination of EFS and custom EC2-based NFS).

nodes.name: Triboo Ops/Dev Team
nodes.notes: Internal users managing the platform services.

nodes.name: AWS VPN
nodes.notes: IPsec VPN used as the secure entry point for managing all internal platform services.

nodes.name: Mail Server
nodes.notes: Self-managed EC2 instance running email dispatching services.

nodes.name: Monitoring & Config Lambda
nodes.notes: Triggers monitoring, configuration tasks, and alerts.

edges.notes: Ingest analytical data for Business Intelligence
